title: No hardcoded API keys
clauseText: API keys and access keys must never be hardcoded in source code. They must be loaded from environment variables or a secrets manager so they can be rotated without code changes.
exampleViolations: assigning an API key to a string literal in source code
committing a cloud provider access key into a repository

title: No plaintext passwords
clauseText: Passwords must never be stored or hardcoded in plaintext. Credentials must be supplied via secure configuration, and stored passwords must be salted and hashed.
exampleViolations: a password assigned as a literal string in code
storing user passwords in plaintext in a dataset or config file

title: No committed private keys
clauseText: Private keys and certificates must never be committed to source control. They must be stored in a secure key store or secrets manager with restricted access.
exampleViolations: a PEM private key block (-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----) in a file
checking an SSH or TLS private key into the repository

title: No tokens or secrets in logs
clauseText: Access tokens, bearer tokens, and session secrets must not be written to logs or telemetry, where they could be exposed to anyone with log access.
exampleViolations: logging a bearer or JWT token
printing an OAuth access token to console or log output

title: No embedded connection-string credentials
clauseText: Database and service connection strings containing usernames and passwords must not be hardcoded in source. They must be injected through secure configuration.
exampleViolations: a connection string with an inline password in source code
embedding database credentials in a committed config file
